response.justification: Bogdan is a broad, experienced polyglot engineer with genuinely relevant high-throughput distributed systems work (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and current hands-on exposure to agentic tooling (MCP code generators, AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq), which matches the AI-native baseline this role demands. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from his skills and every listed role, his high-throughput experience is in the niche DLang ecosystem and ended in 2020, and the resume shows little concrete evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, or operational/on-call ownership at scale. Worth a conversation if the Python gap is deemed learnable, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-native experience: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq
Real high-throughput distributed systems background (millions of requests/sec, low-latency async I/O at Sociomantic)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js across product, infra, and open-source work, with a craftsmanship/TDD mindset
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume — the role's primary must-have language
Little demonstrated staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or operational/on-call ownership
Most relevant high-throughput work was in DLang and ended in 2020; recent roles skew toward smaller-scale full-stack product work
